schools.summary: Gill: the preceding book is concluded with the death of Joshua, and this very properly follows.
Keil & Delitzsch: Israel asking the LORD who should go up first.

themes: after the death of Joshua... the children of Israel asked the LORD... Who shall go up for us against the Canaanites first
a people seeking God's direction when their leader is taken
in all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths (Prov. 3:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord answered by an articulate voice, probably by Urim and Thummim.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD saying, Judah shall go up; I have delivered the land into his hand.

themes: the LORD said, Judah shall go up: behold, I have delivered the land into his hand
God appointing the royal tribe of the Messiah to lead, the victory granted
the Lion of the tribe of Juda... hath prevailed (Rev. 5:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Judah and Simeon were brethren by both father and mother, and their inheritances lay near one another; Simeon went with him.

themes: Judah said unto Simeon his brother, Come up with me into my lot... and I likewise will go with thee into thy lot
the strength of brotherly help among God's people
two are better than one... if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow (Eccl. 4:9-10)

schools.summary: Gill: Judah went up from the southern parts northward, called a going up.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD delivering the Canaanites and Perizzites, ten thousand slain.

themes: Judah went up; and the LORD delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand
the victory God's gift to those who go at his bidding
through God we shall do valiantly: for he... shall tread down our enemies (Ps. 60:12)

schools.summary: Gill: Adonibezek, whose name signifies 'lord of Bezek,' was there when they came against the city; they slew the Canaanites and Perizzites.

themes: they found Adonibezek in Bezek: and they fought against him, and they slew the Canaanites and the Perizzites
no power of the wicked stands when God moves against it
the LORD bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought (Ps. 33:10)

schools.summary: Gill: his view was likely to reach Jebus or Jerusalem, a fortified city, but he was pursued and overtaken; they cut off his thumbs and great toes.

themes: Adonibezek fled; and they pursued after him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and his great toes
none escaping the reckoning their own deeds have earned
with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again (Matt. 7:2)

schools.summary: Gill: his conscience accused him for what he had done to others, and he was obliged to acknowledge he was righteously dealt with.
Clarke: the threescore and ten kings he had so treated.

themes: Threescore and ten kings... gathered their meat under my table: as I have done, so God hath requited me
the justice of God owned even by a pagan tyrant
whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap (Gal. 6:7)

schools.summary: Gill: this accounted for their carrying Adonibezek thither; done in the times of Joshua, when its king was taken and slain; they smote it and set it on fire.

themes: the children of Judah had fought against Jerusalem, and had taken it... and set the city on fire
even great victories incomplete where the work is not pressed home
the danger of leaving a conquest unfinished

schools.summary: Gill: this followed the taking of Bezek and the carrying of its king to Jerusalem.
Keil & Delitzsch: Judah going down to fight the Canaanites in mountain, south, and valley.

themes: the children of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites, that dwelt in the mountain, and in the south, and in the valley
a commendable diligence to possess the whole inheritance
I press toward the mark for the prize (Phil. 3:13-14)

schools.summary: Gill: Hebron was first taken by Joshua, but while he was making other conquests, the Canaanites got back; they slew Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.

themes: Judah went against the Canaanites that dwelt in Hebron... and they slew Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai
the giants once the terror of the spies now overthrown
there we saw the giants... and we were as grasshoppers (Num. 13:33)

schools.summary: Gill: Debir was before called Kirjath-sepher.
Ellicott: Debir, whose conquest is assigned to Joshua; the name meaning 'city of the book.'

themes: from thence he went against the inhabitants of Debir: and the name of Debir before was Kirjathsepher
the resolve to leave no stronghold of the enemy unsubdued
let us go on unto perfection (Heb. 6:1)

schools.summary: Gill: Caleb made this offer for the taking of Kirjath-sepher.
Clarke: the whole account is placed here by way of recapitulation, from Joshua 15:13-19.

themes: And Caleb said, He that smiteth Kirjathsepher, and taketh it, to him will I give Achsah my daughter to wife
a reward stirring the brave to valiant deeds in God's cause
to him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne (Rev. 3:21)

schools.summary: Gill: Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother, took it.
Ellicott: Othniel, Caleb's younger brother, who would become the first judge (Judges 3:9).

themes: And Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother, took it: and he gave him Achsah his daughter to wife
the valiant Othniel, later the first judge, advanced by his courage
be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee a crown of life (Rev. 2:10)

schools.summary: Gill: she moved him to ask of her father a field.
Ellicott: she came as a bride, and he being too modest to ask, she urged him.

themes: she moved him to ask of her father a field: and she lighted from off her ass
a holy ambition, earnest and humble in seeking the fuller portion
ye have not, because ye ask not (James 4:2)

schools.summary: Gill: she asked for springs of water, having been given a south, that is, a dry land.
Ellicott: 'a blessing' means a present; Caleb gave the upper and nether springs.

themes: Give me a blessing: for thou hast given me a south land; give me also springs of water... the upper springs and the nether springs
the soul seeking the springs of living water, not content with the dry land
whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst (John 4:14)

schools.summary: Gill: the posterity of Jethro; though Jethro returned home, Hobab his son, at Moses' persuasion, travelled with Israel; they dwelt among the people.

themes: the children of the Kenite, Moses' father in law, went up... with the children of Judah... and dwelt among the people
the Gentile family joined to God's people and received
the strangers that join themselves to the LORD... will I bring to my holy mountain (Isa. 56:6-7)

schools.summary: Gill: Judah, having subdued the Canaanites in his own lot, went with Simeon into theirs.
Keil & Delitzsch: the city destroyed and called Hormah.

themes: Judah went with Simeon his brother, and they slew the Canaanites that inhabited Zephath... and... called Hormah
the fidelity of brotherly partnership, help returned in turn
a friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity (Prov. 17:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Gaza fell to Judah by lot, but was not till now subdued.
Keil & Delitzsch: Gaza, Askelon, and Ekron taken with their coasts.

themes: Also Judah took Gaza with the coast thereof, and Askelon... and Ekron with the coast thereof
victories that, if not maintained, may be lost again
hold that fast which thou hast, that no man take thy crown (Rev. 3:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the Targum reads, 'the Word of the Lord was for the help of the house of Judah.'
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD with Judah, driving out the mountain, but not the valley.

debates: Why Judah could not drive out the valley
The witnesses agree the cause was not God's power but the people's faith; the iron chariots overawed them, where with God they might have prevailed.
themes: the LORD was with Judah... but could not drive out the inhabitants of the valley, because they had chariots of iron
unbelief limiting God; the iron chariots a stumbling-block to weak faith
is any thing too hard for the LORD? (Gen. 18:14)

schools.summary: Gill: done in Joshua's time, according to the order of Moses, of the fields and parts adjacent; he expelled the three sons of Anak.

themes: they gave Hebron unto Caleb, as Moses said: and he expelled thence the three sons of Anak
God's faithfulness to those who follow him fully, the reward sure
he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever (1 John 2:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Jerusalem lay between Judah and Benjamin, and neither could drive out the Jebusites; they dwelt with Benjamin to that day.

themes: the children of Benjamin did not drive out the Jebusites that inhabited Jerusalem; but the Jebusites dwell with the children of Benjamin
the danger of leaving the unexpelled enemy a settled snare
let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body (Rom. 6:12)

schools.summary: Gill: Bethel lay on the borders of Ephraim and Manasseh, now in the Canaanites' possession; the LORD was with them.

themes: the house of Joseph, they also went up against Bethel: and the LORD was with them
the presence of God the pledge and secret of all triumph
certainly I will be with thee (Exod. 3:12)

schools.summary: Gill: they sent to reconnoitre the place, to observe its passes and where the walls were weakest.
Keil & Delitzsch: the sending to descry Bethel.

themes: the house of Joseph sent to descry Bethel. (Now the name of the city before was Luz.)
faith joined to prudence, using lawful means while leaning on God
be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves (Matt. 10:16)

schools.summary: Gill: those sent to watch saw a man come forth, and sought intelligence of the city.
Clarke: them asking him to show the entrance.

themes: Shew us, we pray thee, the entrance into the city, and we will shew thee mercy
grace held out even to a Canaanite who aided God's people
mercy rejoiceth against judgment (James 2:13)

schools.summary: Gill: he pointed out the entrance with his fingers.
Keil & Delitzsch: the city smitten, but the man and his family let go.

themes: they smote the city with the edge of the sword; but they let go the man and all his family
mercy faithfully kept to the one who aided God's people
the merciful man doeth good to his own soul (Prov. 11:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the man went with his family into the land of the Hittites, not in the land of Canaan, and built a city he called Luz.

themes: the man went into the land of the Hittites, and built a city, and called the name thereof Luz
God's sparing mercy giving a fresh beginning, the rescued life renewed
old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new (2 Cor. 5:17)

schools.summary: Gill: this respects the half tribe of Manasseh in Canaan.
Keil & Delitzsch: Manasseh not driving out Beth-shean and the rest, the Canaanites dwelling in the land.

themes: Neither did Manasseh drive out the inhabitants of Bethshean and her towns... but the Canaanites would dwell in that land
partial obedience being disobedience, the spared enemy a lasting bane
they did not destroy the nations, concerning whom the LORD commanded them (Ps. 106:34)

schools.summary: Gill: Israel was now numerous and able to drive the Canaanites out everywhere, yet did not, but put them to tribute.

themes: when Israel was strong, that they put the Canaanites to tribute, and did not utterly drive them out
covetousness and sloth, not weakness; profit set above obedience
what shall it profit a man, if he gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? (Mark 8:36)

schools.summary: Gill: they did not make them tributaries, but probably made a covenant with them, contrary to the will of God.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Canaanites dwelt in Gezer among them.

themes: Neither did Ephraim drive out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer; but the Canaanites dwelt in Gezer among them
making no league with the enemy God commands to be cast out
thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods (Exod. 23:32)

schools.summary: Gill: Kitron and Nahalol seem to be cities given to the Levites, which perhaps was the reason of their slothfulness; the Canaanites became tributaries.

themes: Neither did Zebulun drive out the inhabitants of Kitron, nor... Nahalol; but the Canaanites dwelt among them
a common slackness spreading from tribe to tribe
evil communications corrupt good manners (1 Cor. 15:33)

schools.summary: Gill: Accho, the same with Ptolemais, and the other cities Asher did not drive out.
Keil & Delitzsch: Asher not driving out the inhabitants of these cities.

themes: Neither did Asher drive out the inhabitants of Accho, nor... Zidon... nor of Aphik, nor of Rehob
the widespread failure of a tribe falling short of its calling
he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much (Luke 16:10)

schools.summary: Gill: they were in a worse condition than those before mentioned, for the Canaanites possessed the country and were masters of the cities; the Asherites dwelt among them.

themes: the Asherites dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land: for they did not drive them out
the degradation of God's people dwelling under the enemy's shadow
be not conformed to this world (Rom. 12:2)

schools.summary: Gill: refers to the places of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath.
Clarke and Ellicott: Naphtali not driving them out, the Canaanites becoming tributaries.

themes: Neither did Naphtali drive out the inhabitants of Bethshemesh, nor... Bethanath; but he dwelt among the Canaanites
compromise leaving the idols' houses in the midst as a snare
their idols were a snare unto them (Ps. 106:36)

schools.summary: Gill: the Amorites forced Dan into the mountainous part of their tribe, not suffering them into the valley.

themes: the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the mountain: for they would not suffer them to come down to the valley
the enemy not subdued rising to master God's people
to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are (Rom. 6:16)

schools.summary: Gill: they would not suffer the Danites into the valley, terrifying them with their iron chariots; the house of Joseph prevailed, so that they became tributaries.

themes: the Amorites would dwell in mount Heres in Aijalon... yet the hand of the house of Joseph prevailed, so that they became tributaries
vigour rolling back the enemy, yet the work left half done
resist the devil, and he will flee from you (James 4:7)

schools.summary: Gill: refers to the going up to Akrabbim and the rock.
Clarke: Akrabbim means 'of scorpions,' likely so called from the number of those creatures there.

themes: the coast of the Amorites was from the going up to Akrabbim, from the rock, and upward
the unexpelled enemy a sting in the land, the conquest unfinished
their poison is like the poison of a serpent (Ps. 58:4)
